sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: LightInTheBox Holding Co., Ltd. (NYSE:LITB) Surges 30% Yet Its Low P/S Is No Reason For Excitement
feed.summary: LightInTheBox Holding Co., Ltd. (NYSE:LITB) recently saw a 30% surge in its share price, but despite this, its low price-to-sales (P/S) ratio of 0.2x suggests caution. The company's revenue growth has been minimal, trailing behind the industry average, which explains the modest P/S ratio. Investors are advised against relying solely on the P/S ratio for investment decisions, as the company's weak revenue growth indicates potential ongoing challenges.

feed.title: Microsoft heads into Q2 earnings with cloud strength, AI spending in focus (MSFT:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Microsoft (MSFT) is scheduled to report its Q2 earnings on January 28, with analysts anticipating strong cloud and enterprise demand to drive solid results. Wall Street projects EPS of $3.92 and revenue of $80.28B, a 15.3% year-over-year increase. Key focuses for investors will be the impact of cloud performance and returns on AI investments, juxtaposed against concerns over increasing capital expenditures and potential profitability headwinds.

feed.title: Forget AI Stocks: This Natural Gas Stock Could Soar on AI Demand
feed.summary: AI data centers require a significant amount of power, driving increased demand for natural gas. EQT Corp. is highlighted as a vertically integrated natural gas producer well-positioned to benefit from this demand, with its low-cost production and strategic infrastructure. The company is actively working on projects to supply gas to power producers and anticipates substantial free cash flow in the coming years.

feed.title: Steel Dynamics Inc (NASDAQ:STLD) Tops Profit Estimates
feed.summary: Steel Dynamics Inc (NASDAQ:STLD) exceeded profit estimates in the fourth quarter of 2025, driven by reduced scrap metal costs and stable pricing in its fabrication segment, despite revenue falling short of expectations. The company saw net income of $266 million for the quarter, and achieved record annual steel shipments. STLD is benefiting from improved flat-rolled steel market conditions, strong long product steel demand, and strategic investments in growth and shareholder returns.

feed.title: W R Berkley Corp (NYSE:WRB) Beats Revenue Expectations
feed.summary: W. R. Berkley Corp (NYSE:WRB) saw its stock rise after exceeding revenue expectations for the fourth quarter of FY25. The company reported strong financial results, including a 21.4% return on beginning-of-year stockholders' equity and record quarterly pre-tax underwriting income. W. R. Berkley also returned significant capital to investors through dividends and share repurchases, with a positive outlook for maintaining expense ratios and cautious optimism for premium growth in insurance segments.

feed.title: Genius Sports (GENI) Valuation Check As BetVision For Tennis Launches With Australian Open
feed.summary: Genius Sports (GENI) has launched BetVision for Tennis, an integrated betting interface, coinciding with the Australian Open. Despite recent stock improvements, the company's valuation is under scrutiny, with analysts suggesting it's 39.9% undervalued at $15.89 per share due to expected long-term earnings growth from interactive content, while others point to its P/S ratio of 3.8x as potentially overvalued compared to industry averages and peers. The article encourages investors to consider both growth potential and market expectations.

feed.title: Evercore ISI Reiterates Underperform on IBM Ahead of Earnings, Sees Results In Line
feed.summary: Evercore ISI has reiterated an Underperform rating on IBM, maintaining a $330 price target ahead of the company's fourth-quarter earnings report. Analyst Amit Daryanani expects IBM to meet or slightly exceed consensus estimates, driven by strong performance in its Infrastructure segment and Consulting. The report suggests investors will also be looking for 2026 guidance, AI bookings, and details on Confluent integration, among other key metrics.

feed.title: Expedia Group: Its Valuation And Fundamentals Still Justify The Rally (NASDAQ:EXPE)
feed.summary: Expedia Group maintains a buy rating, supported by strong Q3 2025 results and a resilient business model. Despite some valuation concerns, the company's forward P/E and dividend discount model targets indicate potential for further upside, with technical analysis suggesting bullish momentum. High liquidity, careful debt management, and a flexible B2B/B2C approach position EXPE well to handle inflation and benefit from travel demand.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Charles Sonsteby Sells Shares of Darden Restaurants Inc (DRI)
feed.summary: Charles Sonsteby, a Director at Darden Restaurants Inc (DRI), sold 3,123 shares of the company on January 23, 2026. This transaction follows a pattern of insider selling over the past year, with no insider buys recorded against 27 sells. The stock was trading at $205.32 on the day of the sale, and is considered fairly valued according to GuruFocus's intrinsic value estimate.

feed.title: Nucor Corp (NYSE:NUE) Lags Estimates
feed.summary: Nucor Corp (NYSE:NUE) saw its stock fall after reporting lower-than-expected results for the fourth quarter of FY25, missing analysts' estimates for both adjusted earnings per share and revenue. Despite the miss, the company's adjusted earnings showed a significant improvement year-over-year, and Nucor repurchased common stock and declared a cash dividend. The company anticipates increased earnings across all operating segments in the first quarter of 2026.

feed.title: Oppenheimer Upgrades W.W. Grainger (GWW), Downgrades Emerson (EMR)
feed.summary: Oppenheimer has upgraded W.W. Grainger (GWW) to Outperform with a $1,250 price target, citing its potential to outpace the U.S. maintenance market. Conversely, Emerson Electric (EMR) and Ametek (AME) were downgraded to Perform due to valuation concerns. Grainger's strong financial health, consistent revenue growth, and robust profitability metrics support its positive outlook, though its premium valuation and some insider selling are noted.

feed.title: Benchmark sees 80% upside on Galaxy Digital, flags Helios AI expansion as underpriced potential
feed.summary: Benchmark reiterated a Buy rating for Galaxy Digital (GLXY) with a maintained $57 price target, citing an 80% upside potential. The firm highlights the recently approved 830 MW expansion capacity at Galaxy's Helios data center campus as a key, underpriced factor for future AI infrastructure and high-performance computing, positioning Galaxy uniquely in the AI data center market. This optionality, alongside existing digital asset operations, underpins the positive outlook.

feed.title: UnitedHealth eyes benefit reductions in Medicare (UNH:NYSE)
feed.summary: UnitedHealth (UNH) plans to reduce Medicare Advantage benefits following what it called "disappointing" reimbursement rates proposed by the Trump administration for 2027. The announcement came after a Q4 report that did not meet Street expectations, causing the company's stock to plunge.

feed.title: Norway Chooses Chunmoo System Over Lockheed Martin's HIMARS
feed.summary: Norway has decided to invest $2 billion in South Korea's Chunmoo multiple rocket launcher system by Hanwha Aerospace, rather than Lockheed Martin's HIMARS, to strengthen its Arctic defense near Russia. This decision was largely due to the Chunmoo system meeting operational requirements and offering a rapid delivery schedule. Despite this setback for Lockheed Martin, the company remains a dominant player in the aerospace and defense sector, showcasing strong profitability and consistent revenue growth, though facing concerns regarding valuation and high debt levels.

feed.title: Up 36%, Is Eli Lilly Still a Buy?
feed.summary: Eli Lilly has seen an impressive 36% stock increase since September, driven by its strong performance in weight loss and diabetes medications like Mounjaro and Zepbound. The company reported significant revenue and EPS growth in Q3 2025 and raised its full-year 2025 revenue guidance to $63 billion to $63.5 billion. Despite its premium valuation, the continued high demand and explosive growth in its core product categories suggest Eli Lilly remains a strong buy for long-term investors.

feed.title: Ford’s Lisa Drake to lead new battery energy storage business
feed.summary: Ford has appointed Lisa Drake, former North American COO, to lead its new battery energy storage business, Ford Energy. Drake stated that this move will help the company maximize the value of its battery manufacturing capabilities. The announcement was made on January 27, 2026.

feed.title: Parker-Hannifin Gears Up to Report Q2 Earnings: What's in the Offing?
feed.summary: Parker-Hannifin (PH) is set to release its Q2 fiscal 2026 earnings on January 29, with revenue and EPS expected to increase by 6.3% and 9.5% respectively. The company's performance is anticipated to be driven by strong demand in its Aerospace Systems and Diversified Industrial segments, as well as benefits from the acquisition of Curtis Instruments and its Win Strategy. Despite a positive Zacks Rank, the Earnings ESP suggests it may not conclusively beat estimates this quarter.

feed.title: Sysco Posts Better-than-Expected Earnings
feed.summary: Sysco Corp. reported fiscal second-quarter adjusted earnings of 99 cents per share, surpassing Wall Street expectations of 98 cents. The food distributor posted revenue of $20.76 billion, which slightly fell short of analysts' average estimate of $20.8 billion. The company's net earnings for the quarter were $389 million.

feed.title: First Bancorp: Fourth Quarter Earnings Overview
feed.summary: First Bancorp reported strong financial results for its fourth quarter, with a net income of $87.1 million and earnings of 55 cents per share, surpassing analyst predictions. The company's total revenue for the quarter reached $319.6 million, and full-year profits hit $344.9 million, or $2.15 per share. These figures highlight a successful financial period for the San Juan-based institution.

feed.title: ArcelorMittal Kryvyi Rih to suspend blooming shop in Q2 2026 after losing EU market due to CBAM
feed.summary: ArcelorMittal Kryvyi Rih, the Ukrainian subsidiary of ArcelorMittal, will suspend operations at its blooming shop in Q2 2026. This decision is primarily due to the EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), which eliminates their access to the EU market for a significant portion of their steel output, and high electricity costs in Ukraine, which have doubled since the war began. These factors have rendered the blooming shop's operation economically unviable.

feed.title: Dollar General Corp (DG) Shares Up 3.47% on Jan 26
feed.summary: Dollar General Corp (DG) saw its shares rise by 3.47% on January 26, reaching an intraday high of $152.35 before closing at $152.02. This places the stock just below its 52-week high, significantly above its 52-week low. Analyst forecasts suggest a potential downside from the current price, with an average target of $139.09, while GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $137.65.

feed.title: UnitedHealth Earnings: Shares Fall Further, Despite 2026 Profit Guidance in Line With Expectations
feed.summary: UnitedHealth Group's shares fell significantly following a disappointing Medicare Advantage rate notice, despite the company's 2026 EPS guidance being in line with expectations. Morningstar maintains its fair value estimate of $427, viewing the stock as moderately undervalued, but highlights the company's high uncertainty rating due to ongoing regulatory challenges in its MA and PBM businesses. Investors may face a multiyear wait for a significant profit turnaround.

feed.title: Salesforce wins $5.6B US Army contract for national security technology
feed.summary: Salesforce Inc. has secured a 10-year, $5.6 billion Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract with the US Army, executed through its subsidiary Computable Insights. This agreement aims to provide technology and data solutions to the Department of War, leveraging Salesforce’s cloud-based CRM and data management tools through its Missionforce National Security program. The contract is expected to accelerate procurement, reduce costs, and improve operational efficiency for the Army, integrating disparate data sources into a unified platform.

feed.title: Amazon Go is shutting down
feed.summary: Amazon announced it is shutting down both its Amazon Go convenience store chain and Amazon Fresh grocery brand due to not achieving a "truly distinctive customer experience with the right economic model needed for large-scale expansion." The company plans to convert some of these locations to its Whole Foods Market brand. This marks an end to a challenging period for Amazon's physical retail presence, despite its "Just Walk Out" technology being widely adopted by third parties.

feed.title: Earnings Preview: Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG) Q4 Earnings Expected to Decline
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG) is expected to report a year-over-year decline in Q4 earnings despite higher revenues when it releases results on February 3. The Zacks Consensus Estimate projects quarterly earnings of $0.24 per share (-4% year-over-year) and revenues of $2.98 billion (+4.9% year-over-year). With a negative Earnings ESP of -1.67% and a Zacks Rank #3, it is difficult to confidently predict if Chipotle will beat the consensus EPS estimate this quarter.

feed.title: Analysts Estimate Amgen (AMGN) to Report a Decline in Earnings: What to Look Out for
feed.summary: Analysts expect Amgen (AMGN) to report a year-over-year decline in earnings for the quarter ended December 2025, despite an anticipated rise in revenues. The Zacks Earnings ESP indicates a potential earnings miss with a negative reading of -0.40%, coupled with a Zacks Rank of #3. Investors are advised to consider other factors beyond just earnings expectations, as an earnings beat or miss does not unilaterally determine stock movement.

feed.title: Ametek (AME) Earnings Expected to Grow: Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Ametek (AME) is expected to report increased earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ended December 2025. The company's Most Accurate Estimate is higher than the Zacks Consensus Estimate, and with a Zacks Rank #2 and a positive Earnings ESP of +0.38%, Ametek is likely to beat the consensus EPS estimate. Investors should consider these factors, alongside other market conditions, ahead of the earnings release on February 3.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Earnings Expected to Grow: What to Know Ahead of Next Week's Release
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is expected to report a year-over-year increase in earnings and revenues for the quarter ended December 2025, with an earnings report anticipated on February 3. The company has a Zacks Earnings ESP of +2.01% and a Zacks Rank of #2 (Buy), indicating a high probability of exceeding consensus EPS estimates. AMD has also beaten consensus EPS estimates in the past four quarters.

feed.title: Merck (MRK) Earnings Expected to Grow: What to Know Ahead of Next Week's Release
feed.summary: Merck (MRK) is expected to report a year-over-year increase in earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ended December 2025. The company's upcoming earnings report, due February 3, anticipates quarterly earnings of $2.04 per share and revenues of $16.19 billion. While the Zacks Earnings ESP suggests a potential positive surprise due to analysts becoming more bullish, Merck's Zacks Rank #4 makes a definitive prediction difficult.

feed.title: $100 Billion Market Cap Hospital Operator HCA Healthcare To Buy Back Stock Worth $10 Billion
feed.summary: HCA Healthcare, a major hospital operator, announced a new $10 billion stock buyback program following its mixed fourth-quarter 2025 financial results. The company reported increased revenues and adjusted earnings, exceeding analyst estimates, but missed revenue consensus. HCA Healthcare also provided its 2026 financial outlook, noting potential adverse impacts from health insurance exchanges and declines in supplemental payments.

feed.title: W.W. Grainger (GWW) Expected to Beat Earnings Estimates: What to Know Ahead of Q4 Release
feed.summary: W.W. Grainger (GWW) is projected to report a year-over-year decline in earnings but higher revenues for Q4 2025, with results expected on February 3. Analysts predict quarterly earnings of $9.43 per share and revenues of $4.4 billion. The company has a Zacks Earnings ESP of +0.53% and a Zacks Rank of #3, indicating a high probability of beating the consensus EPS estimate.

feed.title: Corning Stock Hits Record High on Meta Platforms Deal
feed.summary: Corning Inc. (NYSE:GLW) shares surged by 16.7% to a record high of $110.80 after Meta Platforms signed a multiyear fiber-optic supply deal worth up to $6 billion. This deal will support Meta's U.S. AI data-center expansion, with Corning providing optical fiber, cable, and connectivity hardware and increasing manufacturing capacity. The stock's performance marks its best daily percentage gain since April 2004, and its options are experiencing high trading volume.

feed.title: Earnings Preview: Pfizer (PFE) Q4 Earnings Expected to Decline
feed.summary: Pfizer (PFE) is expected to report a year-over-year decline in Q4 earnings and revenues for the quarter ended December 2025. The company's Earnings ESP is -3.24% and it holds a Zacks Rank #5, suggesting it may not be a strong candidate for an earnings beat despite a history of surpassing EPS estimates. Investors are advised to consider other factors beyond the Earnings ESP and Zacks Rank when evaluating the stock's prospects.

feed.title: ComplianceQuest Recognized as a Leader in the 2026 Inaugural Gartner(R) Magic Quadrant(TM) for Quality Management System Software
feed.summary: ComplianceQuest has been recognized as a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Quality Management System (QMS) Software, specifically noted for its high "Ability to Execute." The company's Enterprise Quality Management System (EQMS), built on the Salesforce platform, was evaluated for its comprehensive vision and execution across its integrated quality management solutions. ComplianceQuest highlights its continued investment in AI and automation, emphasizing how its cloud-native platform supports organizations in navigating regulatory complexities and improving decision-making.

feed.title: Brown & Brown Reports $1.6B in Q4 Revenue, $5.9B for 2025
feed.summary: Brown & Brown, Inc. announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025. The company reported total revenues of $1.6 billion for Q4 2025 and $5.9 billion for the full year 2025, demonstrating significant growth. J. Powell Brown, president and CEO, highlighted the acquisition of Accession, strong revenue growth, double-digit adjusted diluted net income per share growth, and good adjusted margin expansion in 2025.

feed.title: Boeing BBJ ‘Freedom Plane’ Becomes Flying Archives Exhibit
feed.summary: Boeing’s VIP-configured 737-700, dubbed the "Freedom Plane," is now serving as a flying exhibit for the National Archives Foundation. This 26-year-old Boeing Business Jet (BBJ1) will tour the U.S., showcasing historic founding documents. The initiative aims to bring important historical artifacts to a wider audience across the country.

feed.title: Capital One hit with class action over allegedly unpaid cashback rewards
feed.summary: Capital One is facing a class action lawsuit for allegedly failing to provide promised cashback rewards through its Capital One Offers program. The plaintiff, Alan B. McNichols, claims that Capital One regularly underpaid cardholders despite representations that rewards would be available within 45 days. The lawsuit, filed in Virginia federal court, alleges breach of contract, unjust enrichment, and violations of consumer protection acts, seeking damages and injunctive relief for a nationwide class and Connecticut subclass.

feed.title: Sense Labs, Inc. announced that it has received funding from Analog Devices, Inc.
feed.summary: Sense Labs, Inc. announced that it has secured a funding round from Analog Devices, Inc. The investment, made on January 27, 2026, involved Sense Labs issuing convertible preferred shares to the new investor. This funding announcement comes amid other news concerning Analog Devices, including shareholder proposals and board member changes.

feed.title: What to Expect from Fair Isaac's Earnings
feed.summary: Fair Isaac (NYSE: FICO) is set to release its quarterly earnings report on Wednesday, January 28, 2026, with analysts anticipating an EPS of $6.59. Investors will be looking for the company to surpass estimates and provide positive guidance for the next quarter, as market reactions are often driven by future outlook. Despite an EPS beat in the last quarter, Fair Isaac's shares have seen a negative 18.08% performance over the last 52 weeks, potentially leading to bearish sentiment among long-term shareholders.

feed.title: AT&T, Verizon Q4 earnings on deck: What to expect (T:NYSE)
feed.summary: AT&T and Verizon are preparing to release their Q4 earnings, with investor focus primarily on AT&T's wireless subscriber growth and margin figures. Analysts expect AT&T to report an EPS of $0.47 on revenue of $32.88 billion. Verizon's profitability will hinge on headcount reductions and a shift to franchised retail, alongside a new customer-centric strategy to boost investor confidence.

feed.title: Hologic stock hits 52-week high at 75.34 USD
feed.summary: Hologic Inc. stock has reached a 52-week high of $75.34, reflecting a strong performance with a 4.83% increase over the past year and a 13.07% gain in six months. The company, a significant player in medical technology with a $16.77 billion market capitalization, exhibits excellent financial health and has an aggressive share buyback program. This milestone, alongside recent developments like a new breast cancer detection solution and expanded CE marking, indicates investor confidence and potential for continued growth.

feed.title: Looking Into United Airlines Holdings Inc's Recent Short Interest
feed.summary: United Airlines Holdings Inc (NYSE: UAL) has seen a 16.76% decrease in its short interest since the last report, with 12.89 million shares sold short, representing 4.52% of its tradable shares. This indicates a potentially more bullish sentiment from investors. Compared to its peers, United Airlines Holdings has less short interest, averaging 9.23% for similar companies.

feed.title: New Keysight tool speeds wireless medical device testing by over 50%
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies (NYSE: KEYS) has launched a new Wireless Coexistence Test Solution designed to automate and accelerate compliance testing for wireless devices, particularly in regulated markets like medical devices. This platform helps engineers validate device performance in crowded RF environments, reduces test cycle time by over 50%, and aims to improve product safety and regulatory readiness aligned with standards like ANSI C63.27.

feed.title: Keysight Launches Wireless Coexistence Test Solution to Accelerate Compliance and Innovation
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies (NYSE: KEYS) has launched a new Wireless Coexistence Test Solution designed to automate and accelerate the validation of wireless device performance in crowded RF environments. This solution helps engineers meet regulatory requirements like ANSI C63.27, particularly critical in regulated markets such as medical devices, by reducing manual setup time, improving test repeatability, and identifying interference risks earlier in development. It includes predefined test scenarios, integrates a wideband vector signal generator, and is built on OpenTAP for configurable and scalable testing, ultimately streamlining the development cycle and ensuring safer, more reliable wireless devices.

feed.title: PayPal Q4 Preview: Analyst Expects Revenue Beat, In-Line Earnings
feed.summary: JPMorgan analyst Tien-tsin Huang reiterated a Neutral rating on PayPal Holdings (NASDAQ: PYPL), anticipating the company will report Q4 revenue above consensus and in-line adjusted earnings. The analyst highlighted potential downside risk to branded checkout growth assumptions but noted management's target of 8%+ branded growth by 2027. Huang projects 5% branded checkout growth for 2026, assuming strong execution, and expects in-line adjusted earnings due to significant share repurchase spending.

feed.title: Nio brings in former Alibaba exec as part of efforts to ramp up AI push
feed.summary: Nio is intensifying its AI investments by hiring Yang Sheng, a former algorithm expert from Alibaba's DAMO Academy, to lead research on AI large language foundation models for smart driving. This move aligns with CEO William Li's vision to integrate AI across all business operations, from R&D to supply chain, and enhance smart driving capabilities, including a significant increase in computing power and upcoming software updates like Banyan 3.3.0.

feed.title: International Seaways Acquires Tankers International, Expands Into Suezmax Market
feed.summary: International Seaways has acquired full ownership of Tankers International, a leading shipping pool, and is expanding its focus beyond VLCCs to include Suezmax vessels. This strategic move aims to enhance agility and flexibility in a changing market, providing greater efficiency and broader cargo options. The expansion will leverage collective intelligence to maximize earnings for pool members across both VLCC and Suezmax vessel classes.

feed.title: RWA Tokenization Protocol Tenbin Raises $7M Led by Galaxy Digital
feed.summary: Tenbin, a real-world asset tokenization platform, has successfully raised $7 million in a funding round led by Galaxy Digital. The company aims to build a "next-gen" tokenized asset infrastructure to make on-chain assets like gold, FX, and commodities faster, more liquid, and more capable than their off-chain counterparts. This initiative comes as the value of tokenized real-world assets, excluding stablecoins, surpassed $33 billion in 2025.

feed.title: Institutional investors in Invivyd, Inc. (NASDAQ:IVVD) see US$113m decrease in market cap last week, although long-term gains have benefitted them.
feed.summary: Institutional investors in Invivyd, Inc. (NASDAQ:IVVD) experienced a US$113 million decrease in market cap last week due to a 16% drop in holdings value. Despite this, they have benefited from a 397% one-year return. Institutions collectively hold 39% of the company's shares, with hedge funds owning an additional 20%, indicating significant influence over Invivyd's share price.

feed.title: Walmart confirms total blackout for dozens of stores across US - and closures affect opening hours too
feed.summary: Walmart has confirmed the closure of dozens of stores across the US due to Winter Storm Fern, prioritizing the safety of employees and customers. While currently 12 stores are blacked out, hundreds had previously shut down temporarily. The company is also donating $500,000 to disaster relief efforts and providing gift cards to warming centers, as the storm has caused widespread disruption, including 30 deaths and power outages for over 800,000 customers.
